theorem_title: First Dirichlet eigenfunction via the Rayleigh quotient
theorem_statement:
First Dirichlet eigenfunction via the Rayleigh quotient.

Let \(\Omega \subset \mathbb{R}^d\) be a bounded domain with sufficiently regular boundary. Define
\[
\lambda_1
:=
\inf\left\{
\int_\Omega |\nabla u|^2\,dx
:u\in H_0^1(\Omega),\ \int_\Omega u^2\,dx = 1
\right\}.
\]
Then the infimum is attained: there exists \(u_1\in H_0^1(\Omega)\) with
\[
\int_\Omega u_1^2\,dx=1
\]
such that
\[
\lambda_1=\int_\Omega |\nabla u_1|^2\,dx.
\]
Moreover, \(u_1\) satisfies the weak eigenvalue equation
\[
\int_\Omega \nabla u_1\cdot\nabla\varphi\,dx
=
\lambda_1 \int_\Omega u_1\varphi\,dx
\qquad \forall \varphi\in H_0^1(\Omega).
\]

nodes.title: First Dirichlet eigenfunction via the Rayleigh quotient
nodes.informal_statement:
The infimum $\lambda_1 = \inf\{\int_\Omega |
abla u|^2\,dx : u\in H_0^1(\Omega),\ \int_\Omega u^2\,dx=1\}$ is attained by some $u_1\in H_0^1(\Omega)$ with $\int_\Omega u_1^2\,dx=1$, and $u_1$ satisfies $\int_\Omega 
abla u_1\cdot
abla\varphi\,dx = \lambda_1 \int_\Omega u_1\varphi\,dx$ for all $\varphi\in H_0^1(\Omega)$.
nodes.informal_proof_text: The infimum is attained by a minimizer $u_1$ obtained via weak compactness and the compact Sobolev embedding (see node 'attainment'). The minimizer then satisfies the weak eigenvalue equation with multiplier $\lambda_1$, derived via the Euler–Lagrange/Lagrange multiplier argument (see node 'weak_eq').

nodes.title: Attainment of the Rayleigh-quotient infimum
nodes.informal_statement: There exists $u_1\in H_0^1(\Omega)$ with $\int_\Omega u_1^2\,dx=1$ and $\int_\Omega |\nabla u_1|^2\,dx = \lambda_1$.
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Let $(u_n)\subset H_0^1(\Omega)$ be a minimizing sequence: $\int_\Omega u_n^2\,dx=1$ and $\int_\Omega |
abla u_n|^2\,dx 	o \lambda_1$. Since $\|u_n\|_{H_0^1}^2 = \int_\Omega |
abla u_n|^2\,dx 	o \lambda_1$, the sequence is bounded in $H_0^1(\Omega)$. By reflexivity (weak compactness), there exists a subsequence (still denoted $(u_n)$) and $u_1\in H_0^1(\Omega)$ such that $u_nightharpoonup u_1$ weakly in $H_0^1(\Omega)$. Since $\Omega$ is bounded and the embedding $H_0^1(\Omega)\hookrightarrow L^2(\Omega)$ is compact (Rellich–Kondrachov), we can pass to a further subsequence with $u_n	o u_1$ strongly in $L^2(\Omega)$. Hence $\int_\Omega u_1^2\,dx = \lim_{n	o\infty}\int_\Omega u_n^2\,dx = 1$, so $u_1$ is admissible. By weak lower semicontinuity of the $H_0^1$-seminorm, $\int_\Omega |
abla u_1|^2\,dx \le \liminf_{n	o\infty}\int_\Omega |
abla u_n|^2\,dx = \lambda_1$. Since $u_1$ is admissible, also $\lambda_1 \le \int_\Omega |
abla u_1|^2\,dx$. Therefore $\int_\Omega |
abla u_1|^2\,dx = \lambda_1$.
nodes.display_label: Lemma: Attainment (weak compactness + compact embedding + wlsc)
nodes.formalization_rationale: This node is the technical core of the theorem. It chains three non-trivial Lean/Mathlib ingredients—boundedness of the minimizing sequence, Rellich–Kondrachov compact embedding for $H_0^1(\Omega)\hookrightarrow L^2(\Omega)$, and weak lower semicontinuity of the $H^1$-seminorm—into a single tight argument. Formalizing this node in Lean would substantially reduce proof-checking burden for the hardest part of the result.

nodes.title: Minimizer satisfies weak eigenvalue equation with multiplier $\lambda_1$
nodes.informal_statement: The minimizer $u_1$ satisfies $\int_\Omega \nabla u_1\cdot\nabla\varphi\,dx = \lambda_1\int_\Omega u_1\varphi\,dx$ for all $\varphi\in H_0^1(\Omega)$.
nodes.informal_proof_text:
Since $u_1$ minimizes $u\mapsto\int_\Omega|
abla u|^2\,dx$ subject to $\int_\Omega u^2\,dx=1$, the Lagrange multiplier theorem for smooth constraints on Hilbert spaces gives a constant $\mu$ such that $\int_\Omega 
abla u_1\cdot
abla\varphi\,dx = \mu\int_\Omega u_1\varphi\,dx$ for all $\varphi\in H_0^1(\Omega)$. Taking $\varphi=u_1$: $\int_\Omega|
abla u_1|^2\,dx = \mu\int_\Omega u_1^2\,dx = \mu\cdot 1 = \mu$. But from the attainment result, $\int_\Omega|
abla u_1|^2\,dx=\lambda_1$, so $\mu=\lambda_1$. Substituting back yields $\int_\Omega
abla u_1\cdot
abla\varphi\,dx = \lambda_1\int_\Omega u_1\varphi\,dx$ for all $\varphi\in H_0^1(\Omega)$.

nodes.title: Identification of the Lagrange multiplier as λ₁
nodes.informal_statement:
If $u_1 \in H_0^1(\Omega)$ satisfies $\int_\Omega u_1^2\,dx = 1$, $\int_\Omega |
abla u_1|^2\,dx = \lambda_1$, and there exists $\mu \in \mathbb{R}$ such that $\int_\Omega 
abla u_1 \cdot 
abla \varphi\,dx = \mu \int_\Omega u_1 \varphi\,dx$ for all $\varphi \in H_0^1(\Omega)$, then $\mu = \lambda_1$.
nodes.informal_proof_text: Substitute $\varphi = u_1$ in the assumed weak equation to obtain $\int_\Omega |\nabla u_1|^2\,dx = \mu \int_\Omega u_1^2\,dx$. The right-hand side equals $\mu \cdot 1 = \mu$ because $\int_\Omega u_1^2\,dx = 1$. The left-hand side equals $\lambda_1$ by hypothesis. Hence $\mu = \lambda_1$.
nodes.formalization_rationale: This is the key algebraic pinning step: once the Lagrange multiplier existence is granted, the exact value of the multiplier is determined by a single substitution and two scalar equalities. It is a self-contained, finitely checkable identity with no abstract machinery remaining, making it an ideal formal island.

edges.label: provides minimizer $u_1$ with correct norm
edges.explanation: The attainment lemma supplies the admissible minimizer used in the root theorem.

edges.label: provides weak eigenvalue equation
edges.explanation: The Euler–Lagrange lemma, given the minimizer from attainment, completes the root theorem.

edges.label: refined_from
edges.explanation: This refined local claim was carved out from the broader sibling node as provenance, not as a proof dependency.

edges.label: uses
edges.explanation: This refined local claim depends on the broader supporting node it was carved out from.
